title: Science, engineering and production technicians n.e.c.
description: Operate, install, adjust, and maintain integrated computer/communications systems, consoles, simulators, and other data acquisition, test, and measurement instruments and equipment, which are used to launch, track, position, and evaluate air and space vehicles. May record and interpret test data. Operate, test, maintain, or adjust unmanned, automated, servomechanical, or electromechanical equipment. May operate unmanned submarines, aircraft, or other equipment to observe or record visual information at sites such as oil rigs, crop fields, buildings, or for similar infrastructure, deep ocean exploration, or hazardous waste removal. May assist engineers in testing and designing robotics equipment. Build, install, test, or maintain robotic equipment or related automated production systems. (Based on 28 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Compile and interpret results of tests and analyses.
Provide technical support or assistance to chemists or engineers, and train new employees on proper operation of laboratory equipment.
Conduct chemical or physical laboratory tests to assist scientists in making qualitative or quantitative analyses of materials, and collect or prepare solid or fluid samples for analysis.
Set up and conduct chemical experiments, tests, and analyses, using techniques such as chromatography, spectroscopy, or microscopy.
Monitor product quality to ensure compliance with standards and specifications, and develop or conduct programs of sampling and analysis to maintain quality standards.
Write technical reports or prepare graphs or charts to document experimental results and prepare or review professional or technical reports regarding sampling, testing, or recommendations.
Maintain, clean, or sterilize laboratory instruments or equipment, and provide and maintain a safe work environment by participating in safety programs and conducting safety audits.
Conduct geological surveys, participate in prospecting field trips, exploratory drilling, well logging, or underground mine survey programs.
Test and analyze samples to determine their content and characteristics, using laboratory apparatus or testing equipment, and compile, log, or record testing or operational data for review.
Prepare and package environmental samples for shipping or testing, and analyze samples for contaminants or other elements.
Conduct geophysical surveys for potential sites and evaluate and interpret geological data using various measurement instruments.
Operate unmanned automated, servomechanical, or electromechanical equipment, and assist engineers in testing and designing robotics equipment.
Observe students' performance, and record relevant data to assess progress, and prepare lesson materials for educational demonstrations.
Collect geological data from potential sites and operate equipment used to obtain geological or radiation data as required.
Supervise activities of workers who operate equipment to collect data and plan experiments or testing procedures.
Collect and analyze pollution samples and decontaminate field equipment used to test pollutants from soil, air, or water.
Prepare permit applications or review compliance with environmental permits, and brief workers on safety levels in work areas.
Educate the public about fire safety and prevention.
Place radioactive waste into containers for shipping or disposal and evaluate technologies to clean up polluted sites.

Operate nanotechnology compounding, testing, processing, or production equipment in accordance with appropriate standard operating procedures, good manufacturing practices, hazardous material restrictions, or health and safety requirements.
Calculate equipment operating factors, such as radiation times, dosages, temperatures, gamma intensities, or pressures, using standard formulas and conversion tables.
Conduct surveillance testing to determine the safety of nuclear and related equipment.
Monitor nuclear reactor and related equipment performance to identify operational inefficiencies, hazards, or needs for maintenance or repair.
Test plant equipment and other systems to ensure proper operation.
Apply safety tags to equipment needing maintenance and follow policies and procedures to ensure personnel safety.
Modify, devise, or maintain nuclear and related equipment used in operations.
Perform testing, maintenance, repair, or upgrading of accelerator and related systems as directed.
Instruct personnel in radiation safety procedures and demonstrate the use of protective clothing and equipment to ensure safety in work areas.
Communicate and coordinate with maintenance personnel regarding readiness of equipment and support systems, like vacuum or cooling systems.
Measure the intensity and identify the types of radiation in work areas, using radiation detectors or other instruments.
Collect air, water, gas or solid samples for testing to determine radioactivity levels or ensure appropriate radioactive containment and decontamination procedures as necessary.
Establish and implement procedures for the cleanup of hazardous waste, including monitoring hazardous waste cleanup procedures.
Prepare detailed presentations and reports for scientists, engineers, and management regarding technological processes and environmental assessments.
Maintain records of operations, equipment conditions, and contamination tests to ensure compliance with safety and operational standards.
Analyze test results in relation to design specifications and test objectives, and modify or adjust equipment accordingly to improve performance.
Develop or modify experimental techniques and processes for nanotechnology and associated equipment to maintain efficiency and improve outcomes.
Repair test equipment or submit work orders for necessary repairs while maintaining workflow standards and procedures.
Collaborate with scientists or engineers for the design and conduct of experiments related to nanotechnology and its applications.
Prepare equipment inspection schedules, reliability schedules, work plans, or other records; and maintain records of survey data.

Identify required data, data acquisition plans, and test parameters, setting up equipment to conform to these specifications; and monitor computer-controlled test equipment, according to written or verbal instructions.

Prepare specifications, designs, or sketches for machines, components, or systems related to the generation, transmission, or use of mechanical or fluid energy; and draft detail drawing or sketch for drafting room completion or to request parts fabrication by machine, sheet or wood shops.

Provide technical support to other employees regarding mechanical design, fabrication, testing, or documentation; and collaborate with agricultural workers to apply remote sensing information to efforts to reduce negative environmental impacts of farming practices.

Set up mechanical, hydraulic, or electric test equipment in accordance with engineering specifications, standards, or test procedures; inspect, diagnose, maintain, and operate test setups and equipment to detect malfunctions; and make repairs to robots or peripheral equipment, such as replacement of defective circuit boards, sensors, controllers, encoders, or servomotors.

Record and interpret test data on parts, assemblies, and mechanisms, document test results, using cameras, spreadsheets, documents, or other tools, and analyze test data for automotive systems, subsystems, or component parts.

Confer with engineering personnel regarding details and implications of test procedures and results; and consult with remote sensing scientists, surveyors, cartographers, or engineers to determine project needs.

Adjust, repair, or replace faulty components of test setups and equipment; install equipment, such as instrumentation, test equipment, engines, or aftermarket products, to ensure proper interfaces; and modify computer-controlled robot movements.
Operate and calibrate computer systems and devices to comply with test requirements and to perform data acquisition and analysis; and integrate remotely sensed data with other geospatial data.

Test systems or components under simulated operational conditions, performing systems readiness tests and pre- and post-operational checkouts; and test performance of electromechanical assemblies, using test instruments such as oscilloscopes, electronic voltmeters, or bridges.

Fabricate and install parts and systems to be tested in test equipment, using hand tools, power tools, and test instruments; and install electrical or electronic parts and hardware in housings or assemblies, using soldering equipment and hand tools.
Prepare parts sketches and write work orders and purchase requests to be furnished by outside contractors, and prepare documentation or presentations, including charts, photos, or graphs.

Test machines, components, materials, or products to determine characteristics such as performance, strength, or response to stress; and evaluate the efficiency and reliability of industrial robotic systems, reprogramming or calibrating to achieve maximum quantity and quality.
Order new test equipment, supplies, or replacement parts; and maintain test equipment in operational condition by performing routine maintenance or making minor repairs or adjustments as needed.

Record test procedures and results, numerical and graphical data, and recommendations for changes in product or test methods; and write technical reports or documentation, such as deviation reports, testing protocols, and trend analyses.

Calculate required capacities for equipment of proposed systems to obtain specified performance and submit data to engineering personnel for approval; and verify integrity and accuracy of data contained in remote sensing image analysis systems.

Recommend tests or testing conditions in accordance with designs, customer requirements, or industry standards to ensure test validity; and participate in out-of-specification and failure investigations and recommend corrective actions.

Monitor testing procedures to ensure that all tests are performed according to established item specifications, standard test methods, or protocols; and investigate or report questionable test results.
Identify and troubleshoot equipment problems; and evaluate analytical methods and procedures to determine how they might be improved.
Participate in research or testing of computerized applications and robotics; and assist engineers in the design, configuration, or application of robotic systems.

Perform preventive or corrective maintenance on robotic systems or components; install, program, or repair programmable controllers, robot controllers, end-of-arm tools, or conveyors; and maintain service records of robotic equipment or automated production systems.
Train customers or other personnel to install, use, or maintain robots; and train other analysts to perform laboratory procedures and assays.

Complete documentation needed to support testing procedures, including data capture forms, equipment logbooks, or inventory forms; and establish and maintain inventory, records, or documentation systems.
Evaluate new technologies and methods to make recommendations regarding their use; and participate in internal assessments and audits as required.

Inspect parts for surface defects; read blueprints, schematics, diagrams, or technical orders to determine methods and sequences of assembly; and align, fit, or assemble component parts, using hand or power tools, fixtures, templates, or microscopes.

Collect geospatial data, using technologies such as aerial photography, light and radio wave detection systems, digital satellites, or thermal energy systems; and evaluate remote sensing project requirements to determine the types of equipment or computer software necessary to meet project requirements, such as specific image types or output resolutions.

Analyze performance of vehicles or components that have been redesigned to increase fuel efficiency; improve fuel efficiency by testing vehicles or components that use lighter materials; and fabricate new or modify existing prototype components or fixtures.
Install new robotic systems in stationary positions or on tracks; and build or assemble robotic devices or systems.
Develop new robotic technologies and implement environmental impact assessment methods; and prepare or review required method transfer documentation including technical transfer protocols or reports.
Inspect installation sites and evaluate methods for optimizing energy use in production.
Conduct statistical studies to analyze or compare production costs; maintain inventories of robotic production supplies; and develop or test new software.

Inspect and test fire protection or fire detection systems to verify that such systems are installed in accordance with appropriate laws, codes, ordinances, regulations, and standards. Prepare and maintain reports of investigation results, including fire inspections performed, fire code violations observed, and corrective recommendations offered. Analyze evidence and other information to determine the probable cause of fire or explosion, and package collected pieces of evidence in securely closed containers to protect them. Testify in court cases involving fires, suspected arson, and false alarms.

Supervise or direct the work of non-destructive testing trainees or staff, and train personnel in assigned duties including firefighting techniques, hazardous materials response, and related subjects. Evaluate fire station procedures and participate in the training and scheduling of firefighting personnel. Maintain required maps and records and oversee the development of fire safety guidelines.

Inspect properties that store, handle, and use hazardous materials to ensure compliance with laws, codes, and regulations, and recommend changes to fire prevention procedures as necessary. Conduct fire code compliance follow-ups to ensure that corrective actions have been taken in cases where violations were found. Identify corrective actions necessary to bring properties into compliance with applicable fire codes, laws, and standards, and explain these measures to property owners or their representatives.

Conduct internal investigations to determine negligence and violation of laws and regulations by employees, and coordinate efforts with other organizations such as law enforcement agencies. Review blueprints and plans for new or remodeled buildings to ensure the structures meet fire safety codes. Develop and coordinate fire prevention programs, such as false alarm billing and fire inspection reporting.

Perform maintenance and minor repairs on firefighting equipment, including vehicles and write and submit proposals to modify, replace, and repair equipment. Direct the training of firefighters and assign instructors to training classes. Evaluate the performance of firefighting personnel and recommend personnel actions related to disciplinary procedures, performance, or grievances.

emerging_tasks.task: Calibrate, maintain, and repair chemical instrumentation sensing elements and sampling system equipment, using calibration instruments and hand tools.
Document results from radiation and contamination surveys.
Inspect, test, and maintain respiratory protection equipment.
Operate drones to capture aerial footage or photographs of crime scenes for further analysis.
Operate drones to monitor and assess fire conditions, track fire progress, and identify safe access points for firefighters.
Operate, test, and troubleshoot uncrewed aerial systems, commonly known as drones, to ensure optimal performance.
Operate remote sensing equipment on drones to collect data in areas that are difficult to access or require high-resolution imagery.
Program and calibrate drones for specific missions or tasks, ensuring proper functionality and performance.
Deploy and monitor drones for aerial surveillance and assessment of fire situations.
Operate drones for remote inspection of large or hard-to-reach structures, such as wind turbines, bridges, or tall buildings.
